title: podocarpus aristulatus subsp. buchii
intro: Podocarpus aristulatus subsp. buchii, also known as the Yellow Podocarp, is a plant species belonging to the family Podocarpaceae. It is used in traditional medicine, particularly in Polynesian cultures, and is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating skin conditions, and supporting respiratory health. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves are often used in teas, infusions, and topical applications, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
